pages.text: Shared XP exists to prevent progression drift between partners. With exactly two active players, XP gained by one is mirrored to the other so the duo remains aligned in long-term growth.
If only one player is active, XP gain is blocked. This prevents solo farming while the partner is absent and preserves the cooperative progression model of the world.
When one partner dies, dropped XP is suppressed and a 30 second restore timer starts for the dead player. During this pending window, restore status is visible in the HUD.
If the alive partner survives the full window, dead player XP is restored to match the alive partner state. The system rewards stable recovery instead of immediate panic re-engagement.
If the alive partner dies during pending restore, both players are reset to 0 XP. This is intentionally harsh and turns the pending phase into a shared responsibility checkpoint.

$(bold)Recommended execution:$() when pending starts, stop overextending, stabilize position, and secure regroup. $(italic)In AAFT, preserving the pair is usually stronger than forcing one more fight.$()
